headline: NVIDIA and Alibaba team up to advance multimodal AI with Qwen3.5 VLM model.

items.title: Our agreement with the Department of War
items.summary: OpenAI has entered into a contract with the Department of War, outlining key safety protocols, legal protections, and deployment strategies for AI systems in classified environments. This agreement establishes clear boundaries regarding the use and potential risks of AI in sensitive military operations. The focus is on ensuring that AI deployment adheres to stringent safety standards, mitigating risks associated with autonomous decision-making in combat scenarios.
items.why_it_matters: This sets a precedent for AI use in military applications, emphasizing safety and legal oversight, crucial for engineers working on AI in sensitive sectors.

items.title: Building Telco Reasoning Models for Autonomous Networks with NVIDIA NeMo
items.summary: NVIDIA introduces a new approach to developing telco reasoning models using their NeMo framework, aimed at autonomous network management. This development addresses the pressing need for smarter, self-managing networks as telecommunications infrastructure becomes increasingly complex. NVIDIA NeMo provides a platform for creating AI models that can handle tasks such as network optimization and fault detection autonomously.
items.why_it_matters: Leads the way for telecom engineers to leverage AI for network automation, enhancing efficiency and reducing human intervention.

items.title: Develop Native Multimodal Agents with Qwen3.5 VLM Using NVIDIA GPU-Accelerated Endpoints
items.summary: Alibaba has released Qwen3.5 VLM, a new open-source model designed for native multimodal agents with approximately 400 billion parameters. This model utilizes NVIDIA GPU-accelerated endpoints to enhance performance and scalability. It is tailored for applications requiring complex visual and language processing capabilities, making it suitable for a variety of AI-driven interactive systems.

items.title: 5 New Digital Twin Products Developers Can Use to Build 6G Networks
items.summary: NVIDIA unveils five new digital twin products aimed at tackling the challenges of designing, training, and validating AI-native 6G networks. These products provide simulation environments that help developers prototype and test complex network architectures before physical deployment, facilitating the rapid advancement of 6G technologies.

items.title: Maximizing GPU Utilization with NVIDIA Run:ai and NVIDIA NIM
items.summary: NVIDIA introduces tools for optimizing GPU utilization in environments with variable inference workloads, such as those involving large language models (LLMs). NVIDIA Run:ai and NVIDIA NIM provide methods to dynamically allocate GPU resources, ensuring efficient processing of both small and large models without resource wastage.

all_items.summary: Details on OpenAI’s contract with the Department of War, outlining safety red lines, legal protections, and how AI systems will be deployed in classified environments.
